modules.name: Auto Dialer
modules.description: Outbound calling automation module. Contains campaign management and record list management. Accessible via top navigation 'Auto dialer' tab.

features.name: Campaign Management
features.description: Create, manage and monitor outbound calling campaigns. Campaigns can be in states: Preparing, Scheduled, Running, Ready, Completed, Paused, Incomplete.
user_steps: Navigate to Auto Dialer > Campaigns
Click 'Create campaign' button
Fill in General Settings (name, description, timezone, dates, business hours, phone numbers)
Configure Dialer Settings (select Progressive mode and configure mode-specific parameters)
Add agents to Agent List
Upload or select a Record List
Save and launch campaign
features.business_value: Enables teams to run structured outbound calling campaigns with full control over scheduling, staffing, and contact lists.

features.name: Progressive Dialing Mode
features.description: The Progressive campaign automatically initiates calls, allowing agents to connect with contacts who are already on the line, making it a more efficient option compared to Preview mode. Best for small to medium teams with steady flow. The system dials the next contact automatically when an agent becomes available.
user_steps: In Dialer Settings, select 'Progressive' mode tab
Configure Auto Answer with timeout (default 3 sec)
Set Timing: Ringing agent time (30 sec), Wrap-up time (30 sec), Max ring time (25 sec)
Set Dialing Attempts: Default retry period (30 min), Max attempts per record (3)
Enable/disable Answering Machine Detection
Configure System Disposition actions (Retry/Final per outcome)
Configure Agent Disposition options (which outcomes agents can select)
features.business_value: Balances automation with efficiency for small to medium outbound teams. More efficient than Preview mode while maintaining steady agent workload without abandoned calls.
key_differences_from_preview: Progressive has Auto Answer with configurable timeout (3 sec default) - Preview does not
Progressive has Answering Machine Detection (AMD) toggle - Preview does not
Progressive has 'Ringing agent time' setting - Preview has 'Preview time' instead
Progressive system dispositions include 'Answering machine' - Preview has 'Blocked by spam filters' and 'Blocked by robocall analytics' instead
Progressive auto-initiates calls; Preview lets agent review contact info first and manually initiate
Progressive is described as 'more efficient' than Preview but gives agents less control

features.name: Local Presence
features.description: Automatically selects the best outbound business number based on the contact's area code. Makes calls appear local to the prospect, increasing answer rates.
user_steps: In General Settings, assign multiple business numbers with regional labels
Toggle on 'Local Presence'
System automatically matches caller ID to contact area code at call time
features.business_value: Increases answer rates by displaying a local number to prospects instead of an unknown toll-free or out-of-state number.

features.name: Business Hours Scheduling
features.description: Configure per-day calling windows for a campaign. Each day (Sunday-Saturday) can be enabled/disabled with custom AM/PM start and end times, or set to Full Day.
user_steps: In General Settings, scroll to Business Hours section
Enable/disable each day with checkbox
Set start and end times per day
Or toggle 'Full day' for unrestricted daily calling
features.business_value: Ensures calls are placed only during legally and operationally acceptable hours, applied per the campaign timezone.

features.name: DNC Compliance
features.description: Do Not Call list management including National DNC list scrubber and Local DNC list. Prevents dialing numbers on DNC registries.
user_steps: Toggle National DNC list scrubber on/off
Manage Local DNC List: Add more numbers, Replace list, Delete all, Export to CSV
Review DNC count (e.g. 2 NUMBERS)
features.business_value: Ensures compliance with TCPA and DNC regulations, protecting the business from legal liability.

features.name: Answering Machine Detection (AMD)
features.description: Automatically detects when a call reaches an answering machine/voicemail. Available in Progressive mode (and Predictive mode). When enabled, adds 'Answering machine' to system dispositions. Can be configured to Retry or Final.
user_steps: In Dialer Settings (Progressive mode), toggle on 'Answering machine detection' (green toggle)
In System Disposition, configure Answering machine outcome as Retry or Final
features.business_value: Prevents wasting agent time on voicemails and enables intelligent retry logic.

features.name: Auto Answer
features.description: Automatically answers incoming connected calls for agents after a configurable timeout. Available in Progressive mode (not in Preview mode). Default timeout is 3 seconds.
user_steps: In Dialer Settings (Progressive mode), locate Auto Answer section
Set Timeout in seconds (default 3 sec)
features.business_value: Reduces agent response time and streamlines call handling in automated dialing modes.

features.name: System Disposition
features.description: Automated handling rules for call outcomes not controlled by agents. Each outcome can be set to Retry (with period and attempt limits) or Final. Progressive mode includes: Contact busy, Contact no response, Contact abandoned, Invalid number, and Answering machine (when AMD is enabled).
user_steps: In Dialer Settings, scroll to System Disposition section
For each outcome: choose Retry or Final
For Retry outcomes: set retry period (min) and max attempts per number

progressive_dispositions.note: Only shown when AMD is enabled

features.business_value: Automates retry logic and eliminates manual queue management for unreachable contacts.

features.name: Agent Disposition
features.description: Configurable set of call outcome options that agents select after each call. Same options as Preview mode. Each disposition can be marked as Final (removes from queue) or non-Final (remains eligible for retry).
user_steps: In Dialer Settings, scroll to Agent Disposition section
Enable or disable specific disposition options for this campaign
Mark each as Final or not
features.business_value: Standardizes agent call outcomes, drives retry logic, and feeds reporting/analytics.

features.name: Record List Management
features.description: Upload and manage contact lists (CSV format) for use in campaigns. Lists show record count, unique area codes, and coverage score. Same functionality as Preview mode.
user_steps: In campaign creation Step 4 (Record list), upload a CSV file or drag-and-drop
Or select an existing record list from the available lists panel
Download the template for correct CSV format
Review coverage score after list is attached
features.business_value: Enables targeted outreach by attaching specific contact databases to each campaign.

features.name: Agent Workspace
features.description: The agent interface during an active Progressive campaign. Displays wrap-up timer (00:30), contact list, conversation thread, and call recordings. Agents select call outcomes and can optionally add numbers to the Do Not Call list.
user_steps: System auto-dials next contact when agent becomes available
Agent conducts call (conversation thread and recordings visible)
After call ends, wrap-up timer starts (30 sec countdown)
Agent selects call outcome: Successful call, Call back later, Not interested, or Left voicemail
Optionally check 'Add the number to Do not call list'
System auto-dials next contact when wrap-up completes
features.business_value: Provides agents with all necessary context and controls during calls while maintaining efficient call flow.

screens.name: Campaigns List
screens.purpose: Overview of all outbound campaigns with status, agents, records, and coverage score. Entry point for campaign management.

components.label: Create campaign
components.action: Opens campaign creation wizard

components.label: Campaigns table
components.action: Lists all campaigns with Name, Description, Agents, Records, Coverage Score columns

components.label: Status badge
components.action: Shows Preparing / Scheduled / Running / Ready / Completed / Paused / Incomplete per campaign

components.label: Play/Pause
components.action: Start or pause a campaign inline

components.label: Three-dot menu
components.action: Edit or Delete campaign

components.label: National DNC list scrubber
components.action: Toggle DNC scrubber on/off

components.label: Local DNC List
components.action: Manage local DNC numbers with Add more, Replace, Delete all, Export to CSV. Shows count (e.g. 2 NUMBERS)

edit_rules: Running and Paused campaigns allow limited edits only
Edit and Delete available via three-dot menu

screens.name: Campaign General Settings (Step 1)
screens.purpose: Configure campaign name, description, timezone, date range, business hours, and assigned phone numbers including local presence. Identical to Preview mode.

components.label: Campaign name
components.action: Enter campaign title

components.label: Description (Optional)
components.action: Enter campaign description, 0/200 char limit

components.label: Timezone

components.label: Start date
components.action: Set campaign start date

components.label: End date

components.label: Business hours
components.action: Enable/disable each day (Sun-Sat) and set AM/PM start-end times or Full Day

components.label: Business Numbers
components.action: Assign phone numbers: LA Main, LA Sales, NYC, Toronto, San Diego, Toll-Free x2

components.label: Auto-rotation
components.action: Enable number rotation

components.label: Local Presence
components.action: Auto-select best number based on contact area code

components.label: Cancel
components.action: Discard campaign and return to list

components.label: Save

screens.name: Campaign Dialer Settings (Step 2) - Progressive Mode

components.label: Dialer mode selector

components.label: Mode description
components.content: The Progressive campaign automatically initiates calls, allowing agents to connect with contacts who are already on the line, making it a more efficient option compared to Preview mode

components.label: Timeout
components.action: Seconds before auto-answer triggers

components.label: Ringing agent time

components.label: Wrap-up time

components.label: Max ring time

components.label: Default retry period

components.label: Max att. per record
components.action: Maximum total dial attempts per contact

components.label: Answering machine detection

components.label: System disposition

screens.name: Agent Workspace (Progressive Campaign)
screens.purpose: Agent interface during active Progressive campaign calls. Shows wrap-up timer, contact info, conversation thread, call recordings, and disposition selection.

components.label: Wrap-up
components.action: Countdown timer showing remaining wrap-up time (00:30)

components.label: Contact list
components.action: Shows current and queued contacts

components.label: Conversation thread

components.label: Call recordings

components.label: SELECT CALL OUTCOME
components.action: Choose disposition: Successful call, Call back later, Not interested, Left voicemail

components.label: Add the number to Do not call list
components.action: Adds contact number to DNC list

user_flows.name: Create a Progressive Dialer Campaign
user_flows.description: End-to-end flow for setting up a new outbound progressive dialing campaign from scratch.

steps.action: Click 'Create campaign'
Enter campaign name, description (0/200), timezone, start/end dates
Configure business hours per day (Sun-Sat)
Assign business numbers (LA Main, LA Sales, NYC, Toronto, San Diego, Toll-Free) and enable Local Presence and Auto-rotation
Select 'Progressive' dialing mode tab
steps.result: Progressive settings panel opens with mode description

steps.action: Configure Auto Answer with timeout (3 sec default)
Set timing: Ringing agent time (30 sec), Wrap-up time (30 sec), Max ring time (25 sec)
Set dialing attempts: Default retry period (30 min), Max attempts per record (3)
Enable Answering Machine Detection toggle
steps.result: AMD activated, Answering machine added to system dispositions

steps.action: Configure system dispositions (Contact busy: Retry, Contact no response: Retry, Contact abandoned: Retry, Invalid number: Final, Answering machine: Final)
Configure agent disposition options (Successful call, Call back later, Incorrect number, Not interested, Left voicemail - all Final)
Add agents to campaign
Upload CSV or select existing record list
Review coverage score
Save and launch campaign

user_flows.name: Agent Handles Progressive Campaign Call
user_flows.description: Flow from agent perspective when working in a Progressive campaign. System auto-dials; agent handles connected calls.

steps.action: System automatically dials next contact when agent becomes available
Contact answers; call auto-answered after timeout (3 sec)
Agent conducts call, views conversation thread and call recordings
Call ends, wrap-up timer starts (00:30 countdown)
Agent selects call outcome (Successful call / Call back later / Not interested / Left voicemail)
Optionally check 'Add the number to Do not call list'
steps.result: Number added to DNC if checked

steps.action: Wrap-up timer expires or agent proceeds

business_processes.name: Outbound Progressive Dialing Campaign
business_processes.description: End-to-end business process for running a progressive dialing outbound sales or outreach campaign. Progressive mode auto-initiates calls for small to medium teams with steady flow.

steps.name: Create campaign with general settings (name, timezone, dates, hours, numbers)
Configure Progressive dialer mode with auto answer, timing, and retry settings
Enable Answering Machine Detection and configure system dispositions
Upload contact record list (CSV) and validate coverage score
Launch campaign - system begins auto-dialing contacts
System dials next contact when agent becomes available
Contact answers - call auto-answered after timeout
AMD detects answering machine - apply Final disposition
Agent conducts call with live contact
Agent selects disposition outcome during wrap-up
System applies retry/final logic based on disposition
System retries or removes record from queue

outcomes: DNC list updated from agent opt-outs

business_processes.name: DNC Compliance Management
business_processes.description: Process for ensuring outbound calls comply with Do Not Call regulations. Identical to Preview mode.
triggers: Agent marks number as DNC during call

steps.name: Toggle National DNC list scrubber on
Upload or manage Local DNC list (Add more, Replace, Delete all, Export to CSV)
System scrubs campaign record lists against DNC
Agent optionally adds number to DNC during call wrap-up

outcomes: Real-time DNC updates from agent actions

data_entities.name: Campaign
data_entities.description: An outbound calling campaign with configuration, staffing, scheduling and contact list.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Preparing / Scheduled / Running / Ready / Completed / Paused / Incomplete

fields.name: business_hours
fields.description: Per-day schedule (Sun-Sat) with start/end times or Full Day

data_entities.name: Progressive Dialer Settings
data_entities.description: Configuration for how the Progressive auto dialer behaves within a campaign. Key differences from Preview: includes Auto Answer, AMD, and Ringing agent time instead of Preview time.

fields.name: auto_answer_timeout_sec
fields.description: Auto-answer timeout in seconds (NOT present in Preview mode)

fields.name: ringing_agent_time_sec
fields.description: Time agent phone rings (Preview has 'Preview time' instead)

fields.name: max_attempts_per_record
fields.description: Max total dial attempts per contact

fields.name: amd_enabled
fields.description: Answering machine detection on/off (NOT present in Preview mode)

data_entities.name: System Disposition (Progressive)
data_entities.description: Automated outcome handling rule for system-level call results in Progressive mode. Differs from Preview: includes 'Answering machine' but does NOT include 'Blocked by spam filters' or 'Blocked by robocall analytics'.

fields.name: outcome_type
fields.description: Contact busy / Contact no response / Contact abandoned / Invalid number / Answering machine

fields.name: retry_period_min
fields.description: Minutes before retry attempt (for Retry outcomes)

fields.name: max_attempts_per_number
fields.description: Max retries for this outcome (for Retry outcomes)

default_values.note: Only shown when AMD is enabled

data_entities.name: Agent Disposition
data_entities.description: Call outcome option that an agent can select after completing a call. Same options as Preview mode.

fields.name: is_final
fields.description: If true, contact is removed from dial queue

data_entities.name: Record List
data_entities.description: A CSV-based contact list uploaded for use in one or more campaigns.

fields.name: unique_area_codes
fields.description: Number of distinct area codes in the list

fields.name: coverage_score_pct
fields.description: % of area codes covered by assigned local numbers

data_entities.name: Business Number
data_entities.description: A phone number assigned to a campaign for outbound caller ID, optionally labeled by region.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Regional label (e.g. LA Main, LA Sales, NYC, Toronto, San Diego, Toll-Free)

data_entities.name: DNC List
data_entities.description: Do Not Call list for compliance. Includes both National DNC scrubber and Local DNC list management.

fields.name: national_scrubber_enabled
fields.description: Whether National DNC list scrubber is active

fields.name: local_dnc_count
fields.description: Number of numbers in local DNC list (e.g. 2 NUMBERS)

ui_patterns.layout_type: Top navigation bar with dropdown sub-menus. Wizard/stepper for campaign creation (4 steps in left sidebar). Two-column layout on creation forms (settings left, supporting panel right).
ui_patterns.color_scheme: Dark navy/black top nav. White content area. Green accent for active nav items, toggle switches, and brand elements. Blue action buttons. Status badges with color coding.
ui_patterns.component_library: Custom design system. Uses card panels, toggle switches (green when ON), data tables with sortable columns, step-wizard sidebar, date pickers, AM/PM time inputs.
common_patterns: 4-step campaign creation wizard with left sidebar progress tracker (General settings, Dialer settings, Agent list, Record list)
Dialer mode tab selector (Preview | Progressive | Predictive | Agentless)
Right-side resource panel (numbers, record lists) alongside main form
Toggle switches for enable/disable features (green when active)
Inline status badges on list screens
Disposition tables with Retry/Final selectors and retry period inputs
Coverage score metric linking local numbers to contact area codes
Wrap-up countdown timer in agent workspace (00:30)
Three-dot menu for Edit/Delete actions on campaign rows
Call outcome dropdown with DNC checkbox in agent workspace

progressive_vs_preview.summary: Progressive is more efficient than Preview but gives agents less control. Progressive auto-initiates calls; Preview lets agents review contact info first.
progressive_only_features: Auto Answer with configurable timeout (3 sec default)
preview_only_features: Preview time setting (agent review time before call)
Blocked by spam filters system disposition
Blocked by robocall analytics system disposition
Agent manually initiates each call after reviewing contact info
